items.title: Counting steps through time: The rise of modern wearables - GJE
items.summary: Ahead of the 1964 Tokyo Olympics, a Japanese clockmaker began marketing a small, waist-worn device known as the Manpo-kei (万歩計). The idea behind the device was simple: allow users to track their daily step count to encourage higher levels of physical activity at a time when rising convenience culture was reducing everyday movement in Japanese […]
